heading: Treatment of loans with below-market interest rates
label: 7872 Treatment of loans with below-market interest rates
notes: Amendments2017—Subsec. (g)(5). amended par. (5) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows:Pub. L. 115–97“(A) .—In the case of any loan made during any calendar year after 1986 to which paragraph (1) applies, the dollar amount in paragraph (2) shall be increased by the inflation adjustment for such calendar year. Any increase under the preceding sentence shall be rounded to the nearest multiple of $100 (or, if such increase is a multiple of $50, such increase shall be increased to the nearest multiple of $100).In general“(B) .—For purposes of subparagraph (A), the inflation adjustment for any calendar year is the percentage (if any) by which—Inflation adjustment“(i) the CPI for the preceding calendar year exceeds“(ii) the CPI for calendar year 1985.For purposes of the preceding sentence, the CPI for any calendar year is the average of the Consumer Price Index as of the close of the 12-month period ending on September 30 of such calendar year.”2006—Subsec. (g)(6). , added par. (6).Pub. L. 109–222, § 209(b)(1)Subsec. (h). , added subsec. (h). Former subsec. (h) redesignated (i).Pub. L. 109–222, § 209(a)Subsec. (h)(4). struck out heading and text of par. (e). , substituted “forgone” for “foregone” in heading.Pub. L. 104–188, § 1704(t)(58)(B)Subsec. Effective Date of 1985 Amendment, (b), , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–121, title II, § 204(a)99 Stat. 514Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Oct. 11, 1985Oct. 22, 1986“(a)201.—Section“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by section 201 [amending this section] shall apply with respect to loans made after the date of enactment of this Act [].Oct. 11, 1985“(2).—Section 7872 not to apply to certain loansSection 7872 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] shall not apply to loans made on or before the date of the enactment of this Act [] to any qualified continuing care facility pursuant to a continuing care contract. For purposes of this paragraph, the terms ‘qualified continuing care facility’ and ‘continuing care contract’ have the meanings given such terms by section 7872(g) of such Code (as added by section 201).Oct. 11, 1985“(b)202.—SectionThe amendment made by section 202 [amending this section] shall apply to contracts entered into after , in taxable years ending after such date.”June 30, 1985

Effective Date, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title I, § 172(c)98 Stat. 703Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095July 18, 1984Oct. 22, 1986“(1).—In generalExcept as provided in paragraph (2), the amendments made by this section [enacting this section] shall apply to—“(A)term loans made after , andJune 6, 1984“(B)demand loans outstanding after .June 6, 1984“(2).—Exception for demand loans outstanding on , and repaid within 60 days after date of enactmentjune 6, 1984The amendments made by this section shall not apply to any demand loan which—“(A)was outstanding on , andJune 6, 1984“(B)was repaid before the date 60 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [].July 18, 1984“(3).—Exception for certain existing loans to continuing care facilitiesNothing in this subsection shall be construed to apply the amendments made by this section to any loan made before , to a continuing care facility by a resident of such facility which is contingent on continued residence at such facility.June 6, 1984“(4).—Applicable federal rate for periods beforejanuary 1, 1985For periods before , the applicable Federal rate under paragraph (2) of section 7872(f) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954], as added by this section, shall be 10 percent, compounded semiannually.January 1, 1985“(5).—Treatment of renegotiations, etcFor purposes of this subsection, any loan renegotiated, extended, or revised after , shall be treated as a loan made after such date.June 6, 1984“(6).—Definition of term and demand loansFor purposes of this subsection, the terms ‘demand loan’ and ‘term loan’ have the respective meanings given such terms by paragraphs (5) and (6) of section 7872(f) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as added by this section, but the second sentence of such paragraph (5) shall not apply.”

L. 99–514Nov. 28, 1989

children.heading: Treatment of gift loans and demand loans
children.label: a Treatment of gift loans and demand loans
A
children.text: transferred from the lender to the borrower, and

children.label: B
children.text: retransferred by the borrower to the lender as interest.

children.heading: Time when transfers made
children.label: 2 Time when transfers made
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, any forgone interest attributable to periods during any calendar year shall be treated as transferred (and retransferred) under paragraph (1) on the last day of such calendar year.

children.heading: Treatment of other below-market loans
children.label: b Treatment of other below-market loans
A
children.text: the amount loaned, over

children.label: B
children.text: the present value of all payments which are required to be made under the terms of the loan.

children.heading: Obligation treated as having original issue discount
children.label: 2 Obligation treated as having original issue discount

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any below-market loan to which paragraph (1) applies shall be treated as having original issue discount in an amount equal to the excess described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Amount in addition to other original issue discount
children.label: B Amount in addition to other original issue discount
children.text: Any original issue discount which a loan is treated as having by reason of subparagraph (A) shall be in addition to any other original issue discount on such loan (determined without regard to subparagraph (A)).

children.heading: Below-market loans to which section applies
children.label: c Below-market loans to which section applies

children.heading: Gifts
children.label: A Gifts
children.text: Any below-market loan which is a gift loan.

children.label: i
children.text: an employer and an employee, or

children.label: ii
children.text: an independent contractor and a person for whom such independent contractor provides services.

children.heading: Corporation-shareholder loans
children.label: C Corporation-shareholder loans
children.text: Any below-market loan directly or indirectly between a corporation and any shareholder of such corporation.

children.heading: Tax avoidance loans
children.label: D Tax avoidance loans
children.text: Any below-market loan 1 of the principal purposes of the interest arrangements of which is the avoidance of any Federal tax.

children.heading: Other below-market loans
children.label: E Other below-market loans
children.text: To the extent provided in regulations, any below-market loan which is not described in subparagraph (A), (B), (C), or (F) if the interest arrangements of such loan have a significant effect on any Federal tax liability of the lender or the borrower.

children.heading: Loans to qualified continuing care facilities
children.label: F Loans to qualified continuing care facilities
children.text: Any loan to any qualified continuing care facility pursuant to a continuing care contract.

children.heading: $10,000 de minimis exception for gift loans between individuals
children.label: 2 $10,000 de minimis exception for gift loans between individuals

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any gift loan directly between individuals, this section shall not apply to any day on which the aggregate outstanding amount of loans between such individuals does not exceed $10,000.

children.heading: De minimis exception not to apply to loans attributable to acquisition of income-producing assets
children.label: B De minimis exception not to apply to loans attributable to acquisition of income-producing assets
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any gift loan directly attributable to the purchase or carrying of income-producing assets.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: C Cross reference
children.text: For limitation on amount treated as interest where loans do not exceed $100,000, see subsection (d)(1).

children.heading: $10,000 de minimis exception for compensation-related and corporate-shareholder loans
children.label: 3 $10,000 de minimis exception for compensation-related and corporate-shareholder loans

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any loan described in subparagraph (B) or (C) of paragraph (1), this section shall not apply to any day on which the aggregate outstanding amount of loans between the borrower and lender does not exceed $10,000.

children.heading: Exception not to apply where 1 of principal purposes is tax avoidance
children.label: B Exception not to apply where 1 of principal purposes is tax avoidance
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any loan the interest arrangements of which have as 1 of their principal purposes the avoidance of any Federal tax.

children.heading: Special rules for gift loans
children.label: d Special rules for gift loans

children.heading: Limitation on interest accrual for purposes of income taxes where loans do not exceed $100,000
children.label: 1 Limitation on interest accrual for purposes of income taxes where loans do not exceed $100,000

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of subtitle A, in the case of a gift loan directly between individuals, the amount treated as retransferred by the borrower to the lender as of the close of any year shall not exceed the borrower’s net investment income for such year.

children.heading: Limitation not to apply where 1 of principal purposes is tax avoidance
children.label: B Limitation not to apply where 1 of principal purposes is tax avoidance
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any loan the interest arrangements of which have as 1 of their principal purposes the avoidance of any Federal tax.

children.heading: Special rule where more than 1 gift loan outstanding
children.label: C Special rule where more than 1 gift loan outstanding
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), in any case in which a borrower has outstanding more than 1 gift loan, the net investment income of such borrower shall be allocated among such loans in proportion to the respective amounts which would be treated as retransferred by the borrower without regard to this paragraph.

children.heading: Limitation not to apply where aggregate amount of loans exceed $100,000
children.label: D Limitation not to apply where aggregate amount of loans exceed $100,000
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to any loan made by a lender to a borrower for any day on which the aggregate outstanding amount of loans between the borrower and lender exceeds $100,000.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “net investment income” has the meaning given such term by section 163(d)(4).

children.heading: De minimis rule
children.label: ii De minimis rule
children.text: If the net investment income of any borrower for any year does not exceed $1,000, the net investment income of such borrower for such year shall be treated as zero.

children.heading: Additional amounts treated as interest
children.label: iii Additional amounts treated as interest
children.text: In determining the net investment income of a person for any year, any amount which would be included in the gross income of such person for such year by reason of section 1272 if such section applied to all deferred payment obligations shall be treated as interest received by such person for such year.

children.heading: Deferred payment obligations
children.label: iv Deferred payment obligations
children.text: The term “deferred payment obligation” includes any market discount bond, short-term obligation, United States savings bond, annuity, or similar obligation.

children.heading: Special rule for gift tax
children.label: 2 Special rule for gift tax
children.text: In the case of any gift loan which is a term loan, subsection (b)(1) (and not subsection (a)) shall apply for purposes of chapter 12.

children.heading: Definitions of below-market loan and forgone interest
children.label: e Definitions of below-market loan and forgone interest
A
children.text: in the case of a demand loan, interest is payable on the loan at a rate less than the applicable Federal rate, or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a term loan, the amount loaned exceeds the present value of all payments due under the loan.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of interest which would have been payable on the loan for the period if interest accrued on the loan at the applicable Federal rate and were payable annually on the day referred to in subsection (a)(2), over

children.label: B
children.text: any interest payable on the loan properly allocable to such period.

children.heading: Other definitions and special rules
children.label: f Other definitions and special rules
A
children.text: as of the date of the loan, and

children.label: B
children.text: by using a discount rate equal to the applicable Federal rate.

children.heading: Term loans
children.label: A Term loans
children.text: In the case of any term loan, the applicable Federal rate shall be the applicable Federal rate in effect under section 1274(d) (as of the day on which the loan was made), compounded semiannually.

children.heading: Demand loans
children.label: B Demand loans
children.text: In the case of a demand loan, the applicable Federal rate shall be the Federal short-term rate in effect under section 1274(d) for the period for which the amount of forgone interest is being determined, compounded semiannually.

children.heading: Gift loan
children.label: 3 Gift loan
children.text: The term “gift loan” means any below-market loan where the forgoing of interest is in the nature of a gift.

children.heading: Amount loaned
children.label: 4 Amount loaned
children.text: The term “amount loaned” means the amount received by the borrower.

children.heading: Demand loan
children.label: 5 Demand loan
children.text: The term “demand loan” means any loan which is payable in full at any time on the demand of the lender. Such term also includes (for purposes other than determining the applicable Federal rate under paragraph (2)) any loan if the benefits of the interest arrangements of such loan are not transferable and are conditioned on the future performance of substantial services by an individual. To the extent provided in regulations, such term also includes any loan with an indefinite maturity.

children.heading: Term loan
children.label: 6 Term loan
children.text: The term “term loan” means any loan which is not a demand loan.

children.heading: Husband and wife treated as 1 person
children.label: 7 Husband and wife treated as 1 person
children.text: A husband and wife shall be treated as 1 person.

children.heading: Loans to which section 483, 643(i), or 1274 applies
children.label: 8 Loans to which section 483, 643(i), or 1274 applies
children.text: This section shall not apply to any loan to which section 483, 643(i), or 1274 applies.

children.label: A
children.text: any amount treated as transferred or retransferred under subsection (a), and

children.label: B
children.text: any amount treated as received under subsection (b).

children.heading: Special rule for term loans
children.label: 10 Special rule for term loans
children.text: If this section applies to any term loan on any day, this section shall continue to apply to such loan notwithstanding paragraphs (2) and (3) of subsection (c). In the case of a gift loan, the preceding sentence shall only apply for purposes of chapter 12.

children.heading: Time for determining rate applicable to employee relocation loans
children.label: 11 Time for determining rate applicable to employee relocation loans

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any term loan made by an employer to an employee the proceeds of which are used by the employee to purchase a principal residence (within the meaning of section 121), the determination of the applicable Federal rate shall be made as of the date the written contract to purchase such residence was entered into.

children.heading: Paragraph only to apply to cases to which section 217 applies
children.label: B Paragraph only to apply to cases to which section 217 applies
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall only apply to the purchase of a principal residence in connection with the commencement of work by an employee or a change in the principal place of work of an employee to which section 217 applies.

children.heading: Exception for certain loans to qualified continuing care facilities
children.label: g Exception for certain loans to qualified continuing care facilities

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: This section shall not apply for any calendar year to any below-market loan made by a lender to a qualified continuing care facility pursuant to a continuing care contract if the lender (or the lender’s spouse) attains age 65 before the close of such year.

children.heading: $90,000 limit
children.label: 2 $90,000 limit
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall apply only to the extent that the aggregate outstanding amount of any loan to which such paragraph applies (determined without regard to this paragraph), when added to the aggregate outstanding amount of all other previous loans between the lender (or the lender’s spouse) and any qualified continuing care facility to which paragraph (1) applies, does not exceed $90,000.

children.label: A
children.text: the individual or individual’s spouse may use a qualified continuing care facility for their life or lives,

children.label: I
children.text: reside in a separate, independent living unit with additional facilities outside such unit for the providing of meals and other personal care, and

children.label: II
children.text: not require long-term nursing care, and

children.label: ii
children.text: then will be provided long-term and skilled nursing care as the health of such individual or individual’s spouse requires, and

children.label: C
children.text: no additional substantial payment is required if such individual or individual’s spouse requires increased personal care services or long-term and skilled nursing care.

children.label: i
children.text: which are designed to provide services under continuing care contracts, and

children.label: ii
children.text: substantially all of the residents of which are covered by continuing care contracts.

children.heading: Substantially all facilities must be owned or operated by borrower
children.label: B Substantially all facilities must be owned or operated by borrower
children.text: A facility shall not be treated as a qualified continuing care facility unless substantially all facilities which are used to provide services which are required to be provided under a continuing care contract are owned or operated by the borrower.

children.heading: Nursing homes excluded
children.label: C Nursing homes excluded
children.text: The term “qualified continuing care facility” shall not include any facility which is of a type which is traditionally considered a nursing home.

children.label: A
children.text: such amount, multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year in which the taxable year begins, by substituting “calendar year 1985” for “calendar year 2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.heading: Suspension of application
children.label: 6 Suspension of application
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply for any calendar year to which subsection (h) applies.

children.heading: Exception for loans to qualified continuing care facilities
children.label: h Exception for loans to qualified continuing care facilities

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: This section shall not apply for any calendar year to any below-market loan owed by a facility which on the last day of such year is a qualified continuing care facility, if such loan was made pursuant to a continuing care contract and if the lender (or the lender’s spouse) attains age 62 before the close of such year.

children.label: A
children.text: the individual or individual’s spouse may use a qualified continuing care facility for their life or lives,

children.label: i
children.text: in an independent living unit (which has additional available facilities outside such unit for the provision of meals and other personal care), and

children.label: ii
children.text: in an assisted living facility or a nursing facility, as is available in the continuing care facility, and

children.label: C
children.text: the individual or individual’s spouse will be provided assisted living or nursing care as the health of such individual or individual’s spouse requires, and as is available in the continuing care facility.

children.label: i
children.text: which are designed to provide services under continuing care contracts,

children.label: ii
children.text: which include an independent living unit, plus an assisted living or nursing facility, or both, and

children.label: iii
children.text: substantially all of the independent living unit residents of which are covered by continuing care contracts.

children.heading: Nursing homes excluded
children.label: B Nursing homes excluded
children.text: The term “qualified continuing care facility” shall not include any facility which is of a type which is traditionally considered a nursing home.

children.label: A
children.text: regulations providing that where, by reason of varying rates of interest, conditional interest payments, waivers of interest, disposition of the lender’s or borrower’s interest in the loan, or other circumstances, the provisions of this section do not carry out the purposes of this section, adjustments to the provisions of this section will be made to the extent necessary to carry out the purposes of this section,

children.label: B
children.text: regulations for the purpose of assuring that the positions of the borrower and lender are consistent as to the application (or nonapplication) of this section, and

children.label: C
children.text: regulations exempting from the application of this section any class of transactions the interest arrangements of which have no significant effect on any Federal tax liability of the lender or the borrower.

children.heading: Estate tax coordination
children.label: 2 Estate tax coordination
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, any loan which is made with donative intent and which is a term loan shall be taken into account for purposes of chapter 11 in a manner consistent with the provisions of subsection (b).
